title: coelogyne banksii
intro: Coelogyne banksii, also known as the Banks' Coelogyne, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in India. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Coelogyne banksii include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and it is also believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, Coelogyne banksii is often combined with other plants to create remedies for various health issues, and it can be consumed as a decoction or applied topically as a paste or oil.
